articles.title: JPY: Energy shock weighs on outlook – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Lloyd Chan highlights that the Japanese Yen (JPY) may weaken further as the energy shock from Middle East tensions persists and markets delay expectations for a Bank of Japan (BoJ) rate hike to June.

articles.expand: Antje Praefcke at Commerzbank warns that Eurozone PMIs (Purchasing Managers' Index) may slip to or below 50 in April as the Middle East war weighs on orders and production.

articles.title: PBOC leaves loan prime rates unchanged, as expected.
